Meaning.En: A small, shallow bowl-shaped utensil with a handle, used for scooping food or liquids.
Meaning.Example: She used a wooden spoon to stir the soup, then a metal scoop to serve it.

Situation.Vi: Một đầu bếp dùng scoop để múc kem hoặc bột mì từ túi.
Situation.En: A chef uses a scoop to portion ice cream or flour from a bag.

Meaning.En: A piece of news or information that is published or revealed before others, giving the reporter or outlet a competitive advantage.
Meaning.Example: The journalist broke the story as a scoop just hours before the official announcement.

Situation.Vi: Đài truyền hình có scoop về một vụ bê bối chính trị mới.
Situation.En: The TV station got the scoop on a new political scandal.

Meaning.En: To remove something, especially food or liquid, using a scoop or similar tool.
Meaning.Example: He scooped up the spilled rice with a spatula.

Situation.Vi: Người phục vụ scoops kem vào cốc sau khi khách gọi món.
Situation.En: The server scoops ice cream into the glass after the customer places the order.

Meaning.Vi: độc quyền đưa tin, có được tin trước
Meaning.En: To obtain or publish important news before other reporters or outlets.
Meaning.Example: The reporter managed to scoop her colleagues by interviewing the witness first.

Situation.Vi: Phóng viên đã scoop đối thủ bằng cách đăng bài trước giờ phát hành chính thức.
Situation.En: The reporter scooped the competition by publishing the article before the official release.
